title: useTimeout
description: Executes a function after a specified delay, with built-in control to start, stop, or reset.
summary: Delays function execution with control over timeout lifecycle.

`,(0,r.jsx)(n,{name:"use-timeout",code:`"use client";\r
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef } from "react";\r
\r
export function getEnv() {\r
  if (typeof process !== "undefined" && process.env && process.env.NODE_ENV) {\r
    return process.env.NODE_ENV;\r
  }\r
  return "development";\r
}\r
\r
export function useTimeout(callback: (...callbackParams: any[]) => void, delay: number, options: { autoInvoke: boolean } = { autoInvoke: false }) {\r
  const timeoutRef = useRef<number | null>(null);\r
\r
  const start = useCallback(\r
    (...callbackParams: any[]) => {\r
      if (!timeoutRef.current) {\r
        timeoutRef.current = window.setTimeout(() => {\r
          callback(callbackParams);\r
          timeoutRef.current = null;\r
        }, delay);\r
      }\r
    },\r
    [delay]\r
  );\r
  const clear = useCallback(() => {\r
    if (timeoutRef.current) {\r
      window.clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\r
      timeoutRef.current = null;\r
    }\r
  }, []);\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    if (options.autoInvoke) {\r
      start();\r
    }\r
    return clear;\r
  }, [clear, start]);\r
\r
  return { start, clear };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-timeout.ts"})]})}function d(t={}){let{wrapper:e}=t.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...t,children:(0,r.jsx)(u,{...t})}):u(t)}function g(t,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+t+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return w(R);})();
